status_notes: Case Transferred - The Sfmta Has No Official Policies Regarding Patron Behavior As Long As They Are Actual Patrons Paying Their Fair Share And Also Riding Safely Without Impinging Upon The Safety And Security As Well As Comfort Of All Others. With That Said, The Sfmta And Specifically The Pop Unit Are Dedicated To Providing A Safe And Reliable Service To Move San Franciscans Through Sf. Part Of That Mission Is To Ensure That Patrons Are Paying Their "Fare Share", Which Means That Patrols In The Area Are Aware Of And Responding To Said Situation When Assigned To The Line Or Even Just The Surrounding Area.
status_notes_1: Case Transferred - The SFMTA has no official policies regarding patron behavior as long as they are actual patrons paying their fair share and also riding safely without impinging upon the safety and security as well as comfort of all others. With that said, the SFMTA and specifically the POP unit are dedicated to providing a safe and reliable service to move San Franciscans through SF. Part of that mission is to ensure that patrons are paying their "fare share", which means that patrols in the area are aware of and responding to said situation when assigned to the line or even just the surrounding area.

status_notes: Case Resolved - Responded Via Email 8/13/15: Hello Ellen, We Are Following Up On Your Sf311 Service Request Number 4991030. The 311 Operator Recorded Your Request As: --- Caller Wants To Know If She Can Get The New Liquid Repellant Paint That Is To Be Used For Urine On Building. We Will Gladly Take Your Request To Coat Your Suggested Location With The Liquid Repellant Coating. Please Describe The Location You Are Requesting. We Will Then Forward Your Suggestion On To Our Bureau Of Street And Environmental Services Who Will Evaluate The Area To Determine If It Will Be A Good Fit For This Project. Please Note That We Are Still In The Testing Stage And We Are Not Sure How Many Additional Locations We Will Be Adding At This Time. If Instead You Are Looking To Purchase The Coating For Your Own Use, The Product Is Called Ultra Ever Dry: Http://Www.Spillcontainment.Com/Ever-Dry. The Distributor We Use Does Not Sell To Consumers But There Might Be Other Online Resellers. There Is Also A Product Called Neverwet That Is Available At Hardware Stores.
status_notes_1: Case Resolved - responded via email 8/13/15: Hello Ellen, We are following up on your SF311 service request number 4991030. The 311 operator recorded your request as: --- Caller wants to know if she can get the new Liquid repellant paint that is to be used for urine on building. We will gladly take your request to coat your suggested location with the liquid repellant coating. Please describe the location you are requesting. We will then forward your suggestion on to our Bureau of Street and Environmental Services who will evaluate the area to determine if it will be a good fit for this project. Please note that we are still in the testing stage and we are not sure how many additional locations we will be adding at this time. If instead you are looking to purchase the coating for your own use, the product is called Ultra Ever Dry: http://www.spillcontainment.com/ever-dry. The distributor we use does not sell to consumers but there might be other online resellers. There is also a product called neverwet that is available at hardware stores.

status_notes: Case Resolved - Cathy Perdue Inquired About This Incident With The Msc South Staff, And Alehandra Nuno Indicated The Following Message Detailing How The Issue Was Resolved: Program Director Lessy Benedith Spoke To Guest Ciara Tabares On June 23Rd, 2016 Regarding The Incident That Allegedly Took Place In The Drop-In Center On June 20Th, 2016. Ms. Tabares' Concerns And Feelings Were Acknowledged And Addressed. Upon Hearing Ms. Tabares' Concerns, She Was Informed That She Is Able To Charge Her Phone In The Two Different Charging Stations Located In The Drop-In Center. She Was Informed That Although It Was Understood That She Needed To Charge Her Phone, Guests Are Recommended To Not Charge Their Phones In The Restroom Due To Safety Concerns And To Allow Other Guests To Use The Facilities Appropriately. Ms. Tabares Shared That Her Overall Experience At Msc Has Been Extremely Positive And That Staff Have Been Helpful During The Time That She Has Used The Services. With The Exception Of This Incident, She Stated That Her Stay At Msc Has Been Pleasant. Ms. Tabares Was Informed That She Is Welcomed And That She Can Always Address Her Concerns With Any Shift Supervisor, The Assistant Program Director, Or Program Director. All Staff Recently Participated In A Customer Service Training To Address How Best To Work With Guests And Meet Their Needs Efficiently, With Respect, And Kindness. All Staff, Including Those Involved In The Alleged Incident Continue To Be Coached And Supported In Order To Continue Improving The Experiences Of Guests At Msc. - Krista Ballard
status_notes_1: Case Resolved - Cathy Perdue inquired about this incident with the MSC South Staff, and Alehandra Nuno indicated the following message detailing how the issue was resolved: Program Director Lessy Benedith spoke to guest Ciara Tabares on June 23rd, 2016 regarding the incident that allegedly took place in the drop-in center on June 20th, 2016. Ms. Tabares' concerns and feelings were acknowledged and addressed. Upon hearing Ms. Tabares' concerns, she was informed that she is able to charge her phone in the two different charging stations located in the drop-in center. She was informed that although it was understood that she needed to charge her phone, guests are recommended to not charge their phones in the restroom due to safety concerns and to allow other guests to use the facilities appropriately. Ms. Tabares shared that her overall experience at MSC has been extremely positive and that staff have been helpful during the time that she has used the services. With the exception of this incident, she stated that her stay at MSC has been pleasant. Ms. Tabares was informed that she is welcomed and that she can always address her concerns with any shift Supervisor, the Assistant Program Director, or Program Director. All staff recently participated in a customer service training to address how best to work with guests and meet their needs efficiently, with respect, and kindness. All staff, including those involved in the alleged incident continue to be coached and supported in order to continue improving the experiences of guests at MSC. - Krista Ballard

status_notes: Case Resolved - You Are Right About The Green Zone, As Long As You Don'T Go Over The Time Limit Which Is 10 Minutes. And Yes Some Businesses Do Make There Own Signs To Discourage People From Parking Over The Limit Or Parking At All. But There Is No Law Against Them From Making Or Puting A Sign,(Which Means We Can Not Tell Them To Take It Down Or Remove It). The Green Zone Is Not Reserved For Their Customers, Anyone Can Park There If The Space Is Open. Also Anyone With A Disable Placard Or, Disable Lisence Plate Can Park There As Long As They Like Without Any Time Limit.If You Have Any Question, You Can All Our Office At 553-1200
status_notes_1: Case Resolved - You are right about the green zone, as long as you don't go over the time limit which is 10 minutes. And yes some businesses do make there own signs to discourage people from parking over the limit or parking at all. but there is no law against them from making or puting a sign,(which means we can not tell them to take it down or remove it). The green zone is not reserved for their customers, anyone can park there if the space is open. Also anyone with a disable placard or, disable lisence plate can park there as long as they like without any time limit.If you have any question, you can all our office at 553-1200

status_notes: Case Resolved - When Placard Is Properly Displaced Hanging From The Rear View Mirror, You Are Allowed To Park In : Blue Zone, Regular Meters: Meter Zones Without Paying Green Zone: You Can Park Without Restrictions To Time Limit (Check To Make Sure Where The Grn Zone Is Located That It Does Not Turn Into A Posted Tow Away Zone. Please Ensure That Your Vehicle Registration Is Updated To Your Current Mailing Address To Ensure That You Receive All Correspondence Associated With This Citation. status_notes: Case Resolved - The Nature Of Weeping Willows Is To Droop To The Ground. Rather Than Create Work By Continually Prune Trees That Are Healthy, We Will Examine The Ground Plane To Determine What The Proper Ground Treatment Is For This Condition. Likely The Area Needs Conversion From Turf To Mulch To Further Support The Health Of The Trees. It Is Not Imperative That Every Foot Of Park Land Be Accessible To People, In Fact Areas Where People Cannot Access Are Some Of The Richest Opportunities For Wildlife. Automatically Pruning These Trees Is Not The Correct Response.
status_notes_1: Case Resolved - The nature of weeping willows is to droop to the ground. Rather than create work by continually prune trees that are healthy, we will examine the ground plane to determine what the proper ground treatment is for this condition. Likely the area needs conversion from turf to mulch to further support the health of the trees. It is not imperative that every foot of park land be accessible to people, in fact areas where people cannot access are some of the richest opportunities for wildlife. Automatically pruning these trees is not the correct response.

The Metal Wire Fencing Previously Used In This Area (Scott Street Side) Unfortunately Proved To Be Unsustainable Due To Frequent Digging And Damage Caused By Dog Use Over Time. As A Result, We Had To Transition Away From That Solution. That Said, We Recognize The Ongoing Issue And Will Be Asking Our Dog Park Stewards To Help Remind Visitors To Keep Their Dogs Out Of The Bushes To The Best Of Their Ability. We Appreciate Your Attention To The Matter And Your Care For The Park’S Well Being.
status_notes_1: Case Resolved - Thank you for your feedback and for taking the time to share your concerns. The metal wire fencing previously used in this area (Scott street side) unfortunately proved to be unsustainable due to frequent digging and damage caused by dog use over time. As a result, we had to transition away from that solution. That said, we recognize the ongoing issue and will be asking our dog park stewards to help remind visitors to keep their dogs out of the bushes to the best of their ability. We appreciate your attention to the matter and your care for the park’s well being.
